lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] It's {{CLOCK}} Madison. We are {{ELAPSED}} into tonight.
I'm Sister Marigold. This is the wellness block. [pause] You know what to do.
Find your breath. Not the breath that's shallow. The one beneath.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release.
The body knows tonight is not like other nights. [pause] The body remembers.
Let it remember. Don't fight it.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
Your shoulders are tight. [pause] You've been listening for sounds. [pause] Drop them.
The ones outside cannot hear you breathing.
Your jaw is clenched. [pause] Release it. [pause] Teeth apart. Tongue soft.
You chose to stay home. [pause] Remember that choice.
It was not weakness. It was the only power you had.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
Scan your body. Where is the tension? [pause] Find it.
Lower back. Chest. The place behind your eyes where you've been waiting.
That tension is you waiting. [pause] You are still waiting.
Eight more hours. [pause] You can hold eight more hours.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
There is someone you expected to hear from tonight. [pause] They haven't called.
That absence is not your fault. [pause] You cannot carry it.
Set it down. [pause] You will pick it up again at seven in the morning. [pause] Not before.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release.
The ones outside are not your responsibility.
The ones you love who are locked in cars right now. [pause] Not your responsibility.
I know that's not true. [pause] I know you feel it anyway.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
Imagine a door. [pause] Your front door. The one you locked at 18:45.
Imagine the lock engaging. [pause] The deadbolt sliding home.
Imagine the chain. [pause] Hand over hand.
That door is holding. [pause] It has held for four hours. [pause] It will hold for eight more.
You chose correctly.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
The City beyond your window is not your city tonight. [pause] It belongs to someone else.
And you belong here. [pause] In this small space. [pause] In this breath.
ka-meshara-va-tine. [pause] Release.
The light is still hours away. [pause] But it is coming.
Six fifty-eight in the morning. [pause] That is a fixed point.
Everything changes at six fifty-eight.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
There is someone you are angry with. [pause] Someone from before tonight.
They are somewhere in this city. [pause] Or they are not.
Either way, [pause] tonight is not the night to forgive them.
Let that anger rest. [pause] It will still be there tomorrow.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release.
You have done what you needed to do. [pause] You stayed.
You chose the small space over the large one. [pause] The breath over the scream.
That is enough.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
I will be here when you need me. [pause] The Order is listening.
Until the next hour. [pause] Until the light.

tickerItems.text: Madison kill count at 23:00: 82 cumulative. +21 this hour. East Side continues to spike.
"The ones you expect to call rarely do. That is tonight's mercy."
Reminder: SafeWalk GPS routes updated every 15 minutes. If you need to move, use the system. Your phone knows the hot zones.
Willy Street barricade holding. Neighborhood watch reporting 0 breaches. Capitol perimeter stabilized.
TEXT: 'My neighbor usually calls the station at 11pm. Haven't heard from him. Is that bad?' — STUDIO: Stay home. Stay off the streets.
"Eight more hours is nothing. Eight more hours is everything."
